description_long: This is a small, traditional wooden house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks painted a faded white, with the gable ends featuring a reddish-brown trim. The house has several multi-pane windows with white frames, some of which are slightly irregular, suggesting age and hand-crafted construction. The front facade features a simple wooden door and two windows, with a small patch of grass and low shrubbery at the base. Moss and signs of weathering are visible on the roof, indicating the house has been exposed to the elements for some time.
description: A small, weathered wooden house with a steep gable roof, white plank walls, reddish-brown trim, and multi-pane windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing a gable end with two multi-pane windows and reddish-brown trim under the roof.
description_view_1: View of the side with the main entry door and a window, both framed in reddish-brown, beneath the large sloped roof.
description_view_2: Opposite gable end with two windows and similar reddish-brown trim, highlighting the house's symmetry.
description_view_3: Front view with the main entry door centered between two windows, a small overhang above, and visible shrubbery at the base.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door with no porch, small overhang, and minimal decorative elements
